{
  "roadmap_5_days": [
    "For the next 5 sessions, before submitting any buy order, pull the composite score and t_score for that instrument. If composite score is below 60 OR t_score is negative, close the order ticket immediately and log the instrument name in a 'blocked list' file. Do not substitute with a 'similar' trade. The goal is to experience the discomfort of not trading and survive it - this is the core behavioral drill that exposes the dip-buying compulsion in real-time without financial consequence.",
    "On any position that moves against you by 3% intraday, set a hard bracket alert at 5% adverse move and write down - before the session ends - the t_score at the time you entered versus the t_score now. If the current t_score is below -1.0, the position must be exited before next open regardless of conviction. Do this manually for 5 sessions to build kinesthetic memory of the score-degradation exit rule before automating it - MANAKSTEEL, NELCAST, and GARUDA all had score degradation visible before the -11% to -13.9% losses were fully realized.",
    "For the next 5 sessions, restrict new position sizing to a maximum of 500 units on any single instrument regardless of liquidity or perceived opportunity. This is not a permanent cap - it is a diagnostic constraint to establish whether your alpha in these sessions comes from scored setups or from the shadow book. At session end, separate your P&L into two buckets: trades with composite score above 60 and positive t_score versus everything else. Five sessions of this data will confirm or deny whether the bifurcated architecture thesis is accurate in live forward conditions."
  ],
  "roadmap_30_days": [
    "Over the next 30 days, formally retire the shadow book by implementing a hard scoring gate at the order-routing layer before broker API submission: any order without a valid composite score above 60 and a non-negative t_score must be blocked programmatically, not discretionarily. Model this on the same infrastructure you already use for child-order fragmentation - the execution layer exists; add the scoring validation upstream of it. IDEA at 527,648 units and HINDCOPPER at 35,000 units with t_score -2.66 are the architectural proof that willpower cannot stop this override; only a pre-execution gate can. Target: zero trades in the sub-60-score, negative-t_score category by day 30.",
    "Rebuild position sizing as a strict tiered formula tied to composite score: score 90 and above receives 100% of your standard unit size (derived from median scored-trade of 38 units scaled appropriately); score 75 to 89 receives 75%; score 60 to 74 receives 50%; anything below 60 is blocked entirely. Any entry with a negative t_score receives 0% allocation regardless of composite score. Apply this formula to every trade in the 30-day window and track the distribution. The goal is to invert the current inverse sizing relationship where GRSE at score 92 receives 251 units while IDEA at score 38.25 receives 527,648 units - this single inversion is worth the majority of the ₹11.96L loss.",
    "Formalize the event-distribution playbook as your exclusive primary book for new capital deployment during this 30-day window. The qualifying criteria are non-negotiable: f_score of 1.0, total_score above 75, Extreme Bullish market_behaviour confirmed at time of entry. STALLION at 5,804 units, ADANIGREEN at 7,000 units, and EMCURE at score 83.75 with a 5-day clean hold represent the evidence base - these are not outliers, they are the system working as designed. Double your standard position size exclusively on this setup class, funded by the capital freed from blocking all sub-60-score entries. Do not open a new position outside this criteria for the full 30 days."
  ],
  "one_move_to_change_everything": "Implement a hard, automated 5% adverse price stop-loss bracket on every open position, combined with an automatic exit trigger when t_score drops below -1.0 from entry score - whichever fires first. The simulation confirms this single architectural change saves ₹15,30,857, which is 128% of your total net loss of ₹11.96L. MANAKSTEEL held to -11%, NELCAST to -13.9%, GARUDA to -9.7%, and STALLION to -7.7% are all trades that would have been closed at 5% with a fraction of the realized damage. This is not a stop-discipline instruction - it must be encoded as a bracket order or automated alert at the point of trade entry, because the behavioral data across all four chunks confirms that once a position is open and in drawdown, the discretionary override seizes control and no manual stop is honored.",
  "one_move_rationale": "The forensic data shows that the average loss of ₹7,111 versus average win of ₹4,648 produces a reward-to-risk ratio of 0.65, which mathematically requires a win rate above 60% to break even - against an actual win rate of 31.8%. The loss magnitude, not the win frequency, is the primary driver of the -₹11.96L outcome. Every percentage point of stop tightening directly compresses the average loss figure, which restructures the payoff distribution even without any change to entry quality or win rate. The simulation delta of ₹15.31L saved from a 20% stop tightening exceeds the total net loss, meaning this one structural change alone would have produced a net-positive account. The dip-buying compulsion and inverse sizing are behavioral problems that a scoring gate can partially address, but the stop protocol is the only mechanism that limits damage after the compulsion has already fired - and the data confirms the compulsion will continue to fire. Stopping the bleed at 5% per trade is the single highest-leverage intervention available given the current behavioral architecture."
}
